$schema-note: RD-0528 COMPILER SELF-HOSTING AUTHORITY LEDGER. A compiler stage listed here is AUTHORITATIVE: its .fungi/WASM is the decider of record for that stage, and the co-located .ts is demoted to a differential SHADOW (kept + its parity test keeps asserting .fungi-WASM === .ts for a bake window; deleted only post-bake — I-3 oracle-before-deletion). A stage is added here ONLY on the OWNER's condition-form nod over its evidence pack (R3 byte-parity + mutation-kill + hash-pin + #105 admission — RD-0528 I-1/I-4). scripts/audit-compiler-stage-twins.mjs reads this ledger and ENFORCES, fail-closed, that every authoritative stage stays `galerina check`-clean AND still differential (a wat-p9-*-parity / self-hosted-* test references it + calls admitAndInstantiate). An authoritative stage that regresses to shadow, or fails check, is RED — the trust-root fail-open this ledger exists to prevent. A malformed ledger is itself RED (deny-by-default); an empty `twins` array means nothing is flipped yet. SEPARATE from the kernel sentinel ledger (rd0361-authoritative-twins.json): flipping a kernel sentinel retires ZERO lines of the .ts compiler — the compiler stages are their own track (RD-0528 §2).
protocol: RD-0528 Phase I compiler self-hosting closure · shadow-bake (keep the .ts one bake window before deleting)
prerequisites: HARD, NOT pre-emptible: I-2 bootstrap-seed (pin a trusted stage0 .fungi->WASM compiler so .fungi compiles .fungi with no .ts) + I-3 oracle-before-.ts-deletion. This gate is the standing enforcement; each flip is owner-gated (I-4: nod -> ledger entry -> shadow-bake window -> post-bake .ts delete). Evidence per stage: (a) R3 byte-parity [wat-p9-*-parity] · (c) mutation-kill non-vacuity · (d) hash-pin + #105 admission [scripts/gather-compiler-stage-hashes.mjs].
